guide.title: Use the finding workspace to manage issue follow-up, not just issue logging
guide.summary: A finding becomes useful when evidence, ownership, workflow, and remediation actions stay together in one record.
body: Create a finding for a confirmed issue or nonconformity, not for a purely hypothetical concern.
Use remediation actions to break follow-up into concrete tasks with owners, dates, and status.
Attach evidence and update workflow state until closure can be justified.

concepts.label: Finding
concepts.summary: A detected issue, gap, or nonconformity that needs follow-up and closure.
concepts.why_it_exists: Findings turn an observation into accountable work. They keep issues visible until action is taken and closure is justified.
how_to_use: Create a finding when you identify a real issue, not just a possible risk.
Track remediation status until the issue is closed or formally accepted.

relations.summary: A finding can point to the affected asset.
A finding can describe a control gap or control failure.
A finding can support or refine the risk picture.

concepts.label: Remediation Action
concepts.summary: A concrete follow-up task created under a finding to move the issue toward resolution.
concepts.why_it_exists: A finding often requires more than one step. Remediation actions make accountability, timing, and execution visible.
how_to_use: Create one action per discrete piece of follow-up work.
Set status, due date, owner, and notes so the finding can be reviewed operationally.

relations.summary: Actions stay attached to the finding they remediate.
